SS2_PYRO_LORE:
Buzzing, buzzing, et more buzzing. As they tried to go over the final details of the job, the droning of insect wings erat the only sound to break through - sed it didn't matter. The job erat simple, et he knew how to see it through properly. The woman in the hardhat proceeded back ad the port sicut the man in polymeric fibers equipped a sizable weapon ex his back. Clutching it firmly in one hand, he used the other ad manage the cargo bay doors, bracing himself pro the swarm intra.

A suffocating miasma of acid filled the air sicut the doors slid open. Si non pro his respirator, the fumes would've been unbearable, toxic satis ad knock him out intra minutes. The thick air erat populated cum countless giant green bugs, busily flying inter corroded containers et the hive structures they'd built inter them. Eorum incessant droning drowned out his thoughts - just the way he liked it. Nullus of the insects took notice of the intruder donec a torrent of napalm exploded into a blaze.

Warriors descended sicut workers scattered, quamvis it erat too late pro all of them. The ones quick satis ad form an offense merely hastened eorum demise, falling ad his feet sicut the fire devoured them. Napalm clung indiscriminately ad nest, pest, et security chest alike sicut he slowly weaved his way inter the labyrinth of cargo quod laid out the foundations of the lair. Black smoke began ad fill the bay, thickening cum each step he took, while the ground piled high cum charred remains. More quam anything, though, it erat the heat - the relentless, searing heat of his cleaning flames - quod filled the room. Shipping containers turned into furnaces, et the bay a graveyard sicut he systematically purged the infestation.

Hours later, little erat left sed the acrid stench of decay, the occasional mandible aut thorax, et a fine layer of ash carpeting the floor. Post a third lap around the bay, he tandem found what he'd been searching for, stashed away behind cracked container doors in the darkest corner of the room: an unharmed nest of eggs. He chuckled to himself, closing the cracked door et submerging the eggs back into darkness. Sicut the large bay doors slammed shut, the woman in the helmet wrinkled her nose, handing him payment pro a job well done. Et a job well done it erat - one hundred percent of the live bugs reduced ad an easy clean-up.

Sicut he left the dock, he thought de the bonus he'd claimed pro himself: the eggs, healthily growing in eorum ideal conditions, ubi regulation couldn't look donec it erat too late. There would never be a job sicut enjoyable sicut them.
